title: Mac desktop app crashes on save when task list contains special Unicode characters from CSV import
problem: The Mac desktop app (approximately v2.3.x) crashes immediately when the user saves a task list that contains special characters — specifically curly quotes, em-dashes, and similar non-ASCII characters introduced via CSV import. The app closes abruptly with a briefly-visible encoding-related error dialog, and unsaved work is lost.
root_cause_hypothesis: The save/serialization function is not handling certain Unicode characters (smart quotes, em-dashes, and potentially other non-ASCII characters commonly introduced by CSV exports from tools like Excel). The encoding error suggests the save path is either using ASCII-only encoding, failing to properly encode UTF-8, or encountering an unhandled exception during character serialization that crashes the app instead of being caught gracefully.
reproduction_steps: Create or import a CSV file containing tasks with special characters: curly/smart quotes (“ ”), em-dashes (—), and other non-ASCII Unicode characters
Import the CSV into TaskFlow (a large number of tasks, e.g. 200, may increase likelihood but the trigger is the character content, not volume)
Attempt to save the task list
Observe: app crashes with a briefly-visible encoding error dialog
environment: Mac desktop app, approximately version 2.3.x. CSV data source likely originated from a tool that uses smart/curly quotes (e.g., Excel, Google Sheets).
impact: User cannot save their task list at all, resulting in repeated data loss. The user has been blocked for days. Any user who imports CSV data containing non-ASCII characters will hit this. There is no known workaround short of manually sanitizing the imported data.
recommended_fix: 1. Investigate the save/serialization path for character encoding — ensure it uses UTF-8 throughout. 2. Add proper error handling around the save operation so encoding failures are caught and reported gracefully rather than crashing the app. 3. Consider sanitizing or normalizing Unicode characters during CSV import (e.g., replacing smart quotes with ASCII equivalents, or ensuring proper encoding at import time). 4. Add an auto-save or recovery mechanism so crashes during save don't result in data loss.
proposed_test_case: Create a task list containing smart quotes (“”‘’), em-dashes (—), and other common non-ASCII characters (accented letters, emoji, symbols like € ©). Verify that saving completes successfully without errors. Additionally, test with a CSV import containing these characters followed by a save operation.
information_gaps: Exact app version number (user said approximately 2.3)
Exact error message from the dialog (it disappears too quickly for the user to read)
Whether the issue reproduces with a small number of tasks containing special characters (isolating volume vs. content as the trigger)
Whether the crash also affects the web app or is Mac-desktop-specific
Console/crash logs from the Mac app that would pinpoint the exact failure location
